purpose: Conformance vectors for the authorization_ref field in the action_ref preimage. authorization_ref = SHA-256(JCS(policy_snapshot_P)) captures the policy state at execution time. A policy update to P' does not invalidate a prior action_ref — it proves which policy was active. Verifiers MUST recompute authorization_ref from the presented policy snapshot and reject if it diverges from the embedded value. pc-004 covers the invocation filter path: a filter operating under current policy P' MUST reject an action_ref issued under P when SHA-256(JCS(P_current)) != authorization_ref_embedded.
reproduce_in_python:
import hashlib, json
def jcs(obj): return json.dumps(obj, separators=(',',':'), sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False)
action_ref = 'sha256:' + hashlib.sha256(jcs(preimage).encode()).hexdigest()
auth_ref = 'sha256:' + hashlib.sha256(jcs(policy_snapshot).encode()).hexdigest()

vectors.name: snapshot_match
vectors.description: Action authorized under policy P, verified against the same policy P. The verifier recomputes authorization_ref = SHA-256(JCS(P)) and finds it matches the embedded value. action_ref is byte-identical. Result: PASS.
vectors.rationale: authorization_ref in the preimage equals SHA-256(JCS(policy_P)). The verifier recomputes over the presented policy snapshot and arrives at the same value. action_ref is therefore byte-identical to the issued reference.

vectors.name: snapshot_mismatch
vectors.description: Action was authorized under policy P, but verification is attempted with P' (updated policy). The verifier recomputes authorization_ref from P' and gets a different hash. The recomputed action_ref diverges from the issued action_ref. Result: FAIL (POLICY_SNAPSHOT_MISMATCH).
vectors.rationale: The verifier MUST recompute authorization_ref from the presented policy snapshot. SHA-256(JCS(P')) != SHA-256(JCS(P)), so the preimage changes, and the recomputed action_ref diverges from the issued action_ref. The verifier MUST reject.

vectors.name: policy_updated_after_action
vectors.description: Action authorized and executed under policy P. Policy is subsequently updated to P'. A verifier operating under P' receives the original action_ref and policy_snapshot_P as historical evidence. The verifier confirms SHA-256(JCS(policy_snapshot_P)) matches the embedded authorization_ref — proving P was active at execution time. The policy update does not invalidate the action_ref. Result: PASS.

verification_steps.step_2: Confirm recomputed authorization_ref == embedded authorization_ref — TRUE

vectors.rationale: authorization_ref binds the policy state at execution time into the action_ref preimage. A subsequent policy update changes P but not the historical authorization_ref hash. The verifier independently confirms P was active when the action_ref was issued.

vectors.name: invocation_filter_policy_changed
vectors.description: Invocation filter path: filter receives an action_ref issued under policy P while the currently active policy is P'. The filter recomputes SHA-256(JCS(P_current=P')) and compares with the authorization_ref embedded in the preimage. Since SHA-256(JCS(P')) != SHA-256(JCS(P)), the filter MUST reject. This is the gate that prevents executing a prior authorization under an updated policy context. Result: FAIL (POLICY_VERSION_CHANGED).
vectors.rationale: The invocation filter MUST gate execution on SHA-256(JCS(P_current)) == embedded authorization_ref. If the policy was updated since the action_ref was issued, the embedded authorization_ref no longer matches the current policy hash. The filter rejects to prevent the action from executing under a policy context that was not in effect when the authorization was granted.
